title: Native Editors: Obsidian + VS Code parity + interconnection (Rust)

markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection generated.

operator_execution_order.order: Operator 2026-06-19: rebuild the Obsidian + VS Code clones as native Rust Handshake tools with FULL feature parity to the existing React surfaces, interconnected with EACH OTHER and with CKC + Loom. Hosted in the WP-KERNEL-011 native shell. Reuse the existing Rust backend (do not rebuild it). As many MTs as needed, each executable by a small no-context local model. Minimal governance paperwork.

scope.summary: Rebuild, as native Rust Handshake tools hosted in the WP-KERNEL-011 shell, the two editor surfaces that currently exist as React (KERNEL-009 'Notes'): (1) the VS-Code-class CODE editor and (2) the Obsidian/Notion-class RICH-TEXT + knowledge editor. Achieve FULL feature parity with what KERNEL-009 already delivered in React, then make them deeply interconnect with each other and with CKC (Pillar 18/19 + KERNEL-005) and Loom (Pillar 7). REUSE the existing Rust backend (handshake_core: knowledge documents, loom, code-nav, CRDT, search) - do NOT rebuild backend storage/APIs. The legacy React editors (app/src) stay as the parity reference until native parity is proven, then are retired. No new future surfaces (Photoshop/Affinity/spreadsheet/engines) are built here; the editors must expose clean extension seams so those plug in later.
forbidden_paths: app/src/** (legacy React editors - parity reference only, do not edit)
src/backend/** (reuse via existing API/IPC only; backend changes are out of scope and must be raised as a typed blocker)
spec_anchors: Product Reference Pillar 3 (Monaco/code), Pillar 4 (Word/rich-text), Pillar 7 (Loom), Pillar 18/19 (Studio/Atelier/CKC)
WP-KERNEL-009 delivered feature set (parity source: MT-244..264 and the Loom/Notes MT family)
acceptance_criteria: CODE editor reaches parity with the existing React Monaco surface: syntax highlighting (tree-sitter), large-file performance, multi-cursor/column-select, find/replace, code folding, minimap/outline, go-to-line, and LSP (completion/hover/go-to-def/references/diagnostics). Proven against the same scenarios the React version passed.

RICH-TEXT editor reaches parity with the existing React Tiptap surface: block document model, headings/lists/tables/quotes/code-blocks, embeds (image/slideshow/album/video), wikilinks + transclusion, slash commands, properties panel, find/replace, daily notes, and save-to-format (HTML/md/txt/JSON).

Obsidian-class knowledge surface parity: local + global graph, folder tree + color labels, tags + tag hubs, pins/favorites/backlinks/unlinked, breadcrumbs, wiki-page projection, canvas board placing real Loom blocks, and block-collection views (table/Kanban/calendar).
Search parity: Loom Search v2 surface (full-text + fuzzy + semantic, graph-blended, results-as-saved-view), find-in-files, and quick-switcher integration - all bound to the existing backend.

INTERCONNECTION (the core deliverable): the two editors and CKC and Loom melt together - shared selection/clipboard/command bus across surfaces; everything is addressable as a Loom block with live backlinks; CKC media/characters/moodboards can be embedded/dragged into notes and canvas and routed to Stage; code and notes can cross-reference; one unified undo + one event-ledger across surfaces. Proven by interconnection scenario tests.

Backend reuse proven: editors read/write through the EXISTING handshake_core APIs (knowledge documents, loom, code-nav, CRDT) with no backend rewrite; any backend gap is a typed blocker, not an in-WP backend change.
Model-vision parity: every editor + knowledge action is exposed via the WP-011 AccessKit surface so a swarm agent can author/edit code and notes and drive the graph/canvas by stable id; proven.

Full-parity proof suite: a runtime suite maps each KERNEL-009-delivered feature to a passing native proof (the parity gate); performance proven on large docs; interconnection proven; zero placeholders/mocks for spec-required runtime behavior.

interconnection_contract.principle: CX-003-VIS / CX-004-VIS: everything melts together, everything accesses everything; handmade primitives share one selection, undo, event ledger, and model-accessibility model.
edges: RichText <-> Code: open a code block/file from a note in the code editor; reference code symbols from notes; shared find/replace and command bus.
Editors <-> Loom: every document/block/canvas node is a Loom block with content hash + backlinks; Loom Search v2 indexes all of it; AI Loom jobs propose tags/links across surfaces.

Editors <-> CKC (Studio/Atelier/KERNEL-005): drag CKC media/characters/worldbuilding/moodboards into a note or canvas as embeds; route a note/selection to a Stage pane; CKC artifacts appear in the same library and search.

Shared substrate: one selection model, one clipboard, one command palette/registry, one undo stack scope policy, one event ledger (Flight Recorder), one AccessKit model-surface across all editor panes.
Future extension seams (designed, NOT built): image/photo editor (Photoshop/Affinity), spreadsheet, mechanical engines - all attach to the same block/selection/event/accessibility substrate.

mt_plan.note: Cluster plan. Each MT-*.json is authored per template and must be executable by a small no-context local model. Toolkit primitives reference the WP-KERNEL-011 MT-001 decision (egui+egui_tiles recommended). Parity is measured against the KERNEL-009 delivered feature set.

clusters.name: Code editor (VS Code parity)
clusters.summary: Text buffer (ropey) + tree-sitter highlighting; large-file virtualization; multi-cursor/column-select; find/replace + replace-in-file; code folding; minimap/outline + go-to-line; gutter (diagnostics/breakpoints); LSP client (tower-lsp-server: completion/hover/def/refs/diagnostics); diff/merge editor (side-by-side + inline); Monaco-parity keymap.

clusters.name: Rich-text editor (Obsidian/Notion parity)
clusters.summary: Block document model (ProseMirror-style on ropey) + cosmic-text WYSIWYG; headings/lists/tables/quotes/code-blocks; embeds (image/slideshow/album/video NodeViews); wikilinks + transclusion; slash commands; properties panel; find/replace; daily-notes/journal; save-to-format (HTML/md/txt/JSON); draft/crash recovery.

clusters.name: Knowledge surface / Obsidian graph (Loom)
clusters.summary: Local + global graph view; folder tree + color labels; tags + tag hubs; pins/favorites/backlinks/unlinked; breadcrumbs; wiki-page projection + editable overlay; canvas board placing real Loom blocks; block-collection views (table/Kanban/calendar).

clusters.name: Search surfaces
clusters.summary: Loom Search v2 native surface (full-text+fuzzy+semantic, graph-blended, faceted, results-as-saved-view); find-in-files (+replace-in-files preview); quick-switcher integration (open docs/blocks/symbols/MTs/WPs/manual/wiki).

clusters.name: Interconnection / melt-together
clusters.summary: Shared selection + clipboard + command bus across editors; everything-is-a-Loom-block addressing + live backlinks; CKC embeds/drag-in (media/characters/moodboards) + route-to-Stage; code<->note cross-references; one unified undo scope policy; one event-ledger (Flight Recorder) across surfaces; designed extension seams for image/spreadsheet/engines.

clusters.name: Backend reuse wiring
clusters.summary: Bind native editors to EXISTING handshake_core APIs: knowledge documents CRUD/history/projection; loom blocks/graph/tags/views; code-nav (symbols/refs/file-lens); CRDT push/pull/conflict. No backend rewrite; gaps raised as typed blockers.

clusters.name: Model-vision/steering parity
clusters.summary: Expose every editor + knowledge + search action through the WP-011 AccessKit surface with stable author_ids; a swarm agent can create/edit a note and code and drive graph/canvas by id; in-process snapshot + action proof.

clusters.name: Parity + interconnection proof
clusters.summary: Parity proof suite mapping each KERNEL-009 delivered feature to a passing native runtime proof (the parity gate); large-document performance proof; interconnection scenario proofs (CKC->note embed, note<->code, Loom backlink/search across surfaces).

assumptions_to_attack: A native Rust rich-text WYSIWYG can reach Tiptap parity (hardest gap; ProseMirror-style model is greenfield - validate early with a vertical slice).
A native code editor can reach Monaco parity incl. LSP + multi-cursor + large files (validate with the heaviest real files).
Interconnection is assumed; prove cross-surface selection/undo/event-ledger and CKC/Loom edges with real scenarios.
Backend reuse is assumed sufficient; any gap must surface as a typed blocker, not a silent backend edit.
Parity is asserted vs proven; every KERNEL-009 feature must map to a passing native proof.
minimum_controls: Parity gate: a runtime proof per KERNEL-009-delivered feature; no placeholders/mocks for spec-required runtime behavior.
Rich-text and code editors each ship an early vertical-slice proof before full cluster build.
Interconnection edges proven by scenario tests, not asserted.
Backend untouched; gaps are typed blockers.
